channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Fri, 17 Jul 2026 13:53:02 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: As wars destroyed schools and libraries across Italy, one Roman official began copying ancient books and helped save centuries of knowledge from disappearing
item.description: War in sixth-century Italy destroyed knowledge, prompting Cassiodorus to act. He founded a monastery where monks copied and translated manuscripts. This work saved religious and secular writings from being lost forever. The Vivarium monastery became a vital center for preserving ancient texts. Cassiodorus's efforts ensured knowledge survived centuries of instability.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Russian recruits survive just 20-30 minutes on Ukraine frontlines: CIA chief explains why
item.description: Russian soldiers face a brief battlefield life expectancy of minutes. AI-powered drones have become specialized, low-cost killing machines on the front. Ukraine's mastery of drone warfare acts as a significant equalizer in the conflict. This technological advantage has helped Ukraine hold off Russia's superior force. Both sides have experienced substantial casualties since the invasion began.
item.dc:creator: TOI defence desk

item.title: Dutch cities are replacing solid concrete surfaces with grass-filled paving blocks that absorb rainwater and help keep urban areas cooler
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Not Philadelphia, this is where the United States started: Inside New Sweden, the long-lost colony that shaped America
item.description: Philadelphia's rich narrative isn't solely tied to its founding fathers' legacy; it also encompasses the short-lived Swedish colony of Nya Sverige. This settlement, which lasted seventeen years, pioneered the design of the log cabin, revolutionizing early American architecture. Furthermore, Swedish settlers introduced Lutheran Christianity and instigated critical early colonial rebellions, underscoring their significant yet brief impact in shaping America's foundations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: He had the money to buy a private jet, yet this billionaire preferred economy flights, took home free packets of salt and pepper from restaurants
item.description: IKEA founder Ingvar Kamprad lived a remarkably simple life despite his vast fortune. He consistently chose flea market clothes and economy travel, setting a personal example. Kamprad reused tea bags and ate at IKEA cafeterias, avoiding expensive dining options. His thrifty habits stemmed from his upbringing in rural southern Sweden. This frugality also shaped IKEA's company culture and employee guidelines.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Several trapped after landslide buries homes in China's Chongqing
item.description: State broadcaster CCTV said a local community worker noticed rocks falling from a hillside at around 8 am local time and issued an emergency warning. Authorities immediately began evacuating more than 60 residents, but the landslide struck at 9.08 am, trapping several people before the evacuation could be completed. Footage aired by CCTV showed a section of the hillside collapsing onto a cluster of riverside homes.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists discovered a plastic-eating fungus in the Amazon rainforest that survives without oxygen and could one day help tackle landfill waste worldwide
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: In New Jersey, cows named Blossom, Misty and Flurry graze beneath solar panels as scientists test whether one field can produce both food and clean energy
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Japan tweaks imperial law but keeps throne male-only, barring women emperors
item.description: The legislation, passed by a large majority in the upper house, permits unmarried male descendants from former imperial branch families to be adopted back into the imperial household. It also allows female members of the imperial family to retain their royal status after marrying commoners, a privilege that was already available to male royals.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Arnold Schwarzenegger: "Just remember, you can't climb the ladder of success with…" - a powerful lesson on ambition, hard work and why success demands action
item.description: Arnold Schwarzenegger emphasized that success requires active effort, not just desire. His quote highlights the gap between ambition and actual work. Progress demands consistent action, which is more crucial than fleeting motivation. Breakthroughs often appear sudden but are built on years of preparation. This principle applies universally to any personal or professional aspiration.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: FBI arrests gangster Nitish Kaushal days after adding him to most wanted list
item.description: The FBI arrested Indian-origin Nitish Kaushal in Vermont as part of a crackdown. Kaushal is wanted for alleged involvement in murder and drug trafficking. He carried out violent acts for the Bhagwanpuria organised crime group. This arrest follows Operation Hard Ball targeting the Bhagwanpuria gang internationally. Federal prosecutors also charged Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar with ordering an assassination.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump's teleprompter operator under federal probe over alleged $90,000 Kalshi insider trading bets
item.description: Donald Trump's teleprompter operator, Gabriel Perez, faces federal investigation for alleged insider trading. He is currently on unpaid leave from the White House after this development. The investigation focuses on trades made on prediction market platform Kalshi. Kalshi detected suspicious activity and alerted regulators, freezing Perez's account. Perez is reportedly cooperating with the ongoing federal inquiry into the matter.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Clavicular pushes back after AOC warns of ‘rabbit holes’ tied to growing looksmaxxing culture
item.description: Clavicular has responded after Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez shared concerns about looksmaxxing during an interview with TMZ. AOC said she worries about the pressures young men may face regarding appearance, while Clavicular argued that looksmaxxing is about improving multiple areas of life, including personality and charisma. Their comments have added to the wider debate around self-image and online influence.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian stabbed 15 times in a mall in US over his religion: Reports
item.description: An Indian man was allegedly stabbed fifteen times inside a Utah shopping mall. The suspect reportedly asked the victim his religion before the violent assault occurred. Authorities arrested the suspect on suspicion of attempted murder and carrying a dangerous weapon. The victim underwent surgery after being transported to a local hospital for treatment. The Indian consulate is in close contact with the victim's family and local authorities.

item.title: Ukrainians protest Zelenskyy's ouster of his popular defense minister
item.description: President Zelenskyy reshuffled his wartime government, impacting key defense and prime ministerial roles. The defense minister's ouster sparked protests, highlighting friction with the military establishment. Ukraine's new prime minister, Serhii Koretskyi, leads the state energy company Naftogaz. These changes occur as Ukraine's fight against Russia continues with ongoing aerial attacks. The personnel overhaul tests Zelenskyy's political authority during the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 'Assassin' of B'desh ex-prez Ziaur Rehman held after 45 yrs
item.description: Former Bangladesh army Major Muzaffar Hossain has been arrested after forty-five years. He is allegedly a mastermind and assassin of President Ziaur Rahman. The arrest occurred in Dhaka's Banani DOHS area late Wednesday night. Hossain was reportedly handed over to the Bangladesh army for court martial. This marks a significant breakthrough for the current Tarique-led BNP government.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: US to revive rule of no green cards for those who use public benefits
Australian father and son were stranded in the outback for 12 hours rescued by a billionaire
item.description: A father and son from Melbourne found themselves lost for twelve hours in the stark South Australian outback after their vehicle sank into mud due to a fierce storm. Their fate caught the attention of billionaire Tim Roberts, who, after hearing their situation at a pub, took to the skies in his helicopter, teaming up with a local to locate and rescue them.

item.title: Quote of the day by Roman poet Virgil: 'If I cannot move Heaven, I will raise Hell', and a celebration of determination
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Richard Nixon: 'Always remember, others may hate you, but those who hate you don't win unless you hate them, and then you destroy yourself" and a lesson on how to deal with haters
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
